what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG sort icon showing two vertical arrows, with the left arrow pointing upward and the right arrow pointing downward.
observations: Uses an inline SVG element with xmlns, viewBox, width, and height set for a 24x24 icon.
Uses no fill and a currentColor stroke, so the icon inherits text color from its context.
Sets stroke-width to 1.5 with round line caps and round line joins.
Draws the full icon using one path element containing both arrow shapes.
standouts: Opposing vertical arrows communicate bidirectional sort behavior.
conventions: Follows common stroke icon conventions: 24x24 viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps, and rounded joins.
Uses absolute SVG path commands with compact whitespace.
Keeps the asset self-contained with no embedded styling or scripts.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present within the SVG itself.
notes: A compact currentColor SVG asset for a sort control, drawn as paired up/down arrows.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component rather than inside this SVG?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a sort icon, and the SVG content depicts opposing vertical arrows consistent with a sort control.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable, color-inheriting sort icon that can fit into a design system icon set.
contribution: Contributes a compact sort affordance asset with currentColor styling and simple stroke geometry that a fused system could keep as a reusable icon substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor stroke SVG icon depicting vertical up and down arrows for sorting.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a sort action in a reusable design system icon asset.
